claim: "Dandelion root is able to kill 98% of cancer cells within 48 hours."

result.claim: "Dandelion root is able to kill 98% of cancer cells within 48 hours."
result.reasoning: The claim that "Dandelion root is able to kill 98% of cancer cells within 48 hours" is refuted by the available evidence. A study titled "Evaluation of aqueous extracts of Taraxacum officinale on growth and invasion of breast and prostate cancer cells" (Paper ID: W2052615154, Credibility Rating: 1.5/5, Study Type: in_vitro, Population Type: cell_culture) directly investigated the effects of dandelion extracts on cancer cells. This study found that while the crude extract of dandelion leaf (DLE) decreased the growth of MCF-7/AZ breast cancer cells, the aqueous extracts of dandelion root (DRE) had *no effect* on the growth of either breast or prostate cancer cell lines (Proposition ID: prop_278582). No evidence was found to support the specific 98% kill rate or the 48-hour timeframe mentioned in the claim. The searches for "Dandelion root kills cancer cells 98% within 48 hours", "dandelion root cancer", and "Taraxacum officinale cancer" did not yield any supporting propositions or chunks.
